source: legacy build_news_schedule texts (verbatim announce/reminder templates from sandbox/news.py, pre-corpus)

articles.text: News this week: the Singapore Government announced the Baby Bonus & Child Development Account. The Government provides a Baby Bonus cash gift of up to S$11,000 per child, plus dollar-for-dollar matching in the Child Development Account (CDA) usable for childcare, preschool, and healthcare expenses.

News this week: a reminder of the Baby Bonus & Child Development Account, which the Singapore Government announced earlier. The Government provides a Baby Bonus cash gift of up to S$11,000 per child, plus dollar-for-dollar matching in the Child Development Account (CDA) usable for childcare, preschool, and healthcare expenses.

News this week: the Singapore Government announced the Large Family Scheme. Families receive up to S$16,000 of additional support for each third or subsequent child: an increased S$10,000 CDA First Step Grant, a S$5,000 MediSave grant for the mother, and S$1,000 per year in LifeSG credits per child until age 6.

News this week: a reminder of the Large Family Scheme, which the Singapore Government announced earlier. Families receive up to S$16,000 of additional support for each third or subsequent child: an increased S$10,000 CDA First Step Grant, a S$5,000 MediSave grant for the mother, and S$1,000 per year in LifeSG credits per child until age 6.
News this week: the Singapore Government announced the Child LifeSG Credits. Parents receive Child LifeSG credits for every child aged 0 to 12, usable for daily household and child-raising expenses.

News this week: a reminder of the Child LifeSG Credits, which the Singapore Government announced earlier. Parents receive Child LifeSG credits for every child aged 0 to 12, usable for daily household and child-raising expenses.

News this week: the Singapore Government announced the Enhanced Paternity Leave. Government-paid paternity leave is doubled from 2 to 4 weeks, with an additional 2 weeks made mandatory for employers from April 2025, so fathers can share early infant care.

News this week: a reminder of the Enhanced Paternity Leave, which the Singapore Government announced earlier. Government-paid paternity leave is doubled from 2 to 4 weeks, with an additional 2 weeks made mandatory for employers from April 2025, so fathers can share early infant care.

News this week: the Singapore Government announced the Shared Parental Leave. A new Shared Parental Leave scheme gives parents up to 10 weeks of additional government-paid leave to share between mother and father — 6 weeks for children born from April 2025, rising to 10 weeks from April 2026.

News this week: a reminder of the Shared Parental Leave, which the Singapore Government announced earlier. A new Shared Parental Leave scheme gives parents up to 10 weeks of additional government-paid leave to share between mother and father — 6 weeks for children born from April 2025, rising to 10 weeks from April 2026.

News this week: the Singapore Government announced the Flexible Work Arrangement Request Guidelines. Tripartite Guidelines require all employers to fairly consider formal requests for flexible work arrangements such as flexi-place, flexi-time, and flexi-load.

News this week: a reminder of the Flexible Work Arrangement Request Guidelines, which the Singapore Government announced earlier. Tripartite Guidelines require all employers to fairly consider formal requests for flexible work arrangements such as flexi-place, flexi-time, and flexi-load.

News this week: the Singapore Government announced the Preschool & Infant Care Subsidies. Preschool and infant care subsidies are enhanced and fee caps lowered at government-supported centres, making full-day childcare more affordable for working parents.

News this week: a reminder of the Preschool & Infant Care Subsidies, which the Singapore Government announced earlier. Preschool and infant care subsidies are enhanced and fee caps lowered at government-supported centres, making full-day childcare more affordable for working parents.

News this week: the Singapore Government announced the Infant Childminding Pilot. A government-supported childminding pilot offers home-based care for infants aged 2 to 18 months, adding a flexible alternative to centre-based infant care.

News this week: a reminder of the Infant Childminding Pilot, which the Singapore Government announced earlier. A government-supported childminding pilot offers home-based care for infants aged 2 to 18 months, adding a flexible alternative to centre-based infant care.
